# guardicore_incident (Data Source)

Retrieves information about an incident by its ID. The `raw_json` attribute contains the full incident object for fields not exposed as typed attributes.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an incident by ID.
data "guardicore_incident" "example" {
  id = "4506a1ba-15d1-4d10-8299-5c10f34975cb"
}

output "incident_typed_fields" {
  value = {
    incident_type = data.guardicore_incident.example.incident_type
    severity      = data.guardicore_incident.example.severity
    start_time    = data.guardicore_incident.example.start_time
    end_time      = data.guardicore_incident.example.end_time
    ended         = data.guardicore_incident.example.ended
    source_ip     = data.guardicore_incident.example.source_ip
  }
}

output "incident_raw_json" {
  value = data.guardicore_incident.example.raw_json
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the incident.

### Read-Only

- `end_time` (Number) The incident end time as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
- `ended` (Boolean) Whether the incident has ended.
- `incident_type` (String) The type of the incident (e.g., `Incident`, `Deception`, `Network Scan`, `Reveal`, `Experimental`).
- `raw_json` (String) The full incident object as a JSON string. Contains all fields from the API response.
- `severity` (String) The severity of the incident. For example: `LOW`, `MEDIUM`, `HIGH`.
- `source_ip` (String) The source IP address of the incident.
- `start_time` (Number) The incident start time as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
